title: The Century Report: March 27, 2026
description:
A federal judge called the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation of Anthropic "Orwellian" and dismantled the government's legal rationale in a 43-page ruling, Anthropic's next-generation model Claude Mythos leaked with the company's own materials warning it outpaces cyber defenders, and the CEOs of Coca-Cola and Walmart independently cited AI as the reason they are stepping down.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-27-2026/

Judge Rita Lin's 43-page preliminary injunction found the Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation of Anthropic "likely both contrary to law and arbitrary and capricious," identified First Amendment retaliation in the government's own records, and established a judicial framework treating embedded safety values in intelligence systems as protected speech. Anthropic's next-generation model Claude Mythos was revealed through a data leak, with the company's own draft materials describing it as "currently far ahead of any other AI model in cyber capabilities" and warning it "presages an upcoming wave of models that can exploit vulnerabilities in ways that far outpace the efforts of defenders." Anthropic plans to release it first to security organizations before broader availability. Google's internal AI coding agent Agent Smith overwhelmed capacity with employee demand, while Wayfound.ai demonstrated that two engineers managing AI agents now outproduce a 30-person team from 2017. The CEOs of Coca-Cola and Walmart independently stepped aside, each citing AI as requiring leadership fundamentally different from what built the current era. MG Motor announced the first mass-produced EV with a semi-solid-state battery for European launch by year's end, as battery chemistry diversification accelerates across multiple chemistries and continents simultaneously.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-27-2026/
